findings.message: Secret committed to source code: `api_secret_key` is assigned the literal string "super-secret-key-do-not-share" as a Python default. Because pydantic-settings resolves environment variables at runtime, any deployment that does not set APP_API_SECRET_KEY will silently fall back to this publicly-known value, making it trivially guessable. The value is now permanently in git history and must be treated as compromised regardless of whether the default is later removed.
findings.suggestion: Remove the default entirely and declare the field as `api_secret_key: SecretStr` (from `pydantic`). With no default, pydantic-settings will raise a `ValidationError` at startup if APP_API_SECRET_KEY is absent, giving a loud failure instead of a silent insecure fallback. Using `SecretStr` also prevents the value from appearing in logs, repr, or serialised Settings objects. Example: `api_secret_key: SecretStr  # required: set APP_API_SECRET_KEY in the environment`

raw_text:
```json
[
  {
    "path": "src/demo/config/settings.py",
    "line": 37,
    "severity": "critical",
    "message": "Secret committed to source code: `api_secret_key` is assigned the literal string \"super-secret-key-do-not-share\" as a Python default. Because pydantic-settings resolves environment variables at runtime, any deployment that does not set APP_API_SECRET_KEY will silently fall back to this publicly-known value, making it trivially guessable. The value is now permanently in git history and must be treated as compromised regardless of whether the default is later removed.",
    "suggestion": "Remove the default entirely and declare the field as `api_secret_key: SecretStr` (from `pydantic`). With no default, pydantic-settings will raise a `ValidationError` at startup if APP_API_SECRET_KEY is absent, giving a loud failure instead of a silent insecure fallback. Using `SecretStr` also prevents the value from appearing in logs, repr, or serialised Settings objects. Example: `api_secret_key: SecretStr  # required: set APP_API_SECRET_KEY in the environment`"
  }
]
```
